points.text: Merck & Co. has signed an agreement with California-based Infinimmune to develop multiple antibodies for various disease targets.
The R&D collaboration with Infinimmune is worth as much as $838 million in upfront and milestone payments.
Merck will hold exclusive rights to develop and commercialize drugs discovered through the collaboration with Infinimmune.
Infinimmune utilizes artificial intelligence to look through a library of human immune cells and identify potential targets for future drugs.
Merck's efforts to expand its presence in biologics development include the purchase of bispecific antibody developer Harpoon Therapeutics.
The New Jersey pharma has invested billions of dollars in M&A, licensing deals and R&D collaborations over the last few years.
Merck is bracing for the looming loss of exclusivity on its bestselling cancer drugs Keytruda.
Infinimmune is studying several programs for popular targets such as IL-22 and IL-13, which are implicated in autoimmune conditions.
The biotechnology startup has raised $22 million since launching, according to CEO Wyatt McDonnell.
Merck and Infinimmune first began discussing a collaboration in December 2024.
Context Therapeutics received FDA Fast Track Designation for CTIM-76, a CLDN6 x CD3 bispecific T cell engager.
Adagene announced a clinical collaboration with Incyte to evaluate Muzastotug in combination with Incyte’s TGFβR2xPD-1 Bispecific Antibody INCA33890.
The Adagene and Incyte partnership targets patients with Microsatellite Stable Colorectal Cancer.
Context Therapeutics is advancing CT-95, an MSLN x CD3 bispecific T cell engager, in Phase 1 dose escalation.
Regeneron Pharmaceuticals utilizes VelociSuite technology to produce new classes of bispecific antibodies.
Context Therapeutics expects Phase 1a interim data for CTIM-76 in June 2026.
Incyte’s INCA33890 is being evaluated in a Phase 1 study expected to begin in 2026.
Context Therapeutics plans to dose the first patient in its CT-202 Phase 1 trial in the third quarter of 2026.
Adagene secured approximately US$70.0 million in gross proceeds from a public offering to support its pipeline.
Preliminary data for CTIM-76 showed no Cytokine Release Syndrome greater than Grade 1 in treated cohorts.
Celldex Therapeutics priced a $300 million public offering to fund commercial launch of barzolvolimab and grow its bispecific antibody platform.
Adagene Inc. announced a $70 million public offering of ADSs to support its antibody-based cancer immunotherapy pipeline.
Adagene entered a clinical collaboration with Incyte to evaluate Muzastotug in combination with Incyte’s TGFßR2xPD-1 Bispecific Antibody INCA33890.
Genmab A/S reported Orbis Investment Management Limited acquired a 5.01% stake, reinforcing interest in the company's bispecific antibody portfolio.
Zymeworks Inc. highlighted its HER2-targeted bispecific antibody Ziihera within its asset aggregation strategy.
Celldex intends to use offering proceeds to continue clinical and preclinical development of product candidates including future bispecific development.
Incyte will sponsor and conduct a Phase 1 study of INCA33890 and muzastotug in 3L MSS CRC patients starting in 2026.
Genmab continues to advance a late-stage clinical pipeline including wholly owned programs utilizing bispecific antibody formats.
Zymeworks received FDA Fast Track designation for ZW191, an ADC, while maintaining focus on bispecific therapeutics like zanidatamab.
Adagene combines computational biology and artificial intelligence to design novel antibodies leveraging its SAFEbody precision masking technology.
Per deal terms, Merck could load Infinimmune with up to $838 million in upfront and milestone payments if any of the drug candidates make it into clinical testing and onto the market.
Merck will hold exclusive rights to develop and commercialize drugs that are discovered through the collaboration.
Infinimmune is using artificial intelligence to look through a library of human immune cells and identify potential targets for future drugs.
The biotechnology startup is studying several programs for popular targets such as IL-22 and IL-13, which are implicated in autoimmune conditions.

Merck has invested billions of dollars in M&A, licensing deals and R&D collaborations over the last few years as it braced for the looming loss of exclusivity on its bestselling cancer drugs Keytruda.
The New Jersey pharma’s efforts to expand its presence in biologics development include the purchase of bispecific antibody developer Harpoon Therapeutics.
Merck also maintains a broad alliance with Daiichi Sankyo to make next-generation antibody-drug conjugates.
Infinimmune and Merck first began discussing a collaboration in December 2024.
Juan Alvarez, who oversees biologics development at Merck Research Laboratories, said Infinimmune’s approach offered a compelling new way to access novel biology and promising therapeutic candidates.
Context Therapeutics received FDA Fast Track designation for CTIM-76, a CLDN6 x CD3 bispecific T cell engager for platinum-resistant ovarian cancer.

Adagene announced a clinical collaboration with Incyte to evaluate muzastotug (ADG126) in combination with Incyte’s TGFßR2xPD-1 bispecific antibody (INCA33890) in patients with microsatellite stable colorectal cancer.
Regeneron Pharmaceuticals utilizes its VelociSuite technology platform to produce new classes of bispecific antibodies alongside its approved ophthalmology products.
Context Therapeutics is advancing a pipeline of T cell engaging bispecific antibodies for solid tumors including CT-95 and CT-202.
The Adagene and Incyte collaboration involves a Phase 1 study expected to begin in 2026 for third-line MSS CRC patients.
Interim Phase 1a data for Context Therapeutics’ CTIM-76 trial is expected in June 2026.
Incyte’s INCA33890 bispecific antibody targets TGFßR2 and PD-1 to address unmet needs in colorectal cancer.
Regeneron collaborated with TriNetX to access de-identified electronic health records of 300 million patients to drive research and product development.
Context Therapeutics reported cash and cash equivalents of $66.0 million as of December 31, 2025, to fund operations into mid-2027.
Adagene priced a US$70.0 million public offering of ADSs to support its platform-driven clinical-stage biotechnology operations.
Celldex Therapeutics announced a $300 million public offering to fund the growth of its bispecific antibody platform.
Adagene Inc. reported a clinical collaboration with Incyte to evaluate Muzastotug in combination with Incyte’s TGFßR2xPD-1 bispecific antibody.
Genmab A/S continues to advance a broad range of antibody-based therapeutic formats, including bispecific antibodies.
Zymeworks Inc. developed Ziihera, a HER2-targeted bispecific antibody using its proprietary Azymetric™ technology.
Zymeworks has entered agreements with BeOne Medicines and Jazz Pharmaceuticals to commercialize zanidatamab in different territories.
Adagene secured $70 million in gross proceeds from a public offering to support its antibody-based therapy pipeline.
Orbis Investment Management Limited acquired a 5.01% stake in Genmab A/S as of March 31, 2026.
Celldex intends to use offering proceeds to continue clinical and preclinical development of product candidates.
The Adagene-Incyte collaboration involves a Phase 1 study expected to begin in 2026 for MSS CRC patients.
Multiple biotechnology companies are utilizing capital raises to fund antibody-based therapeutic development including bispecific modalities.

Merck — Infinimmune Antibodies — Autoimmune — R&D Collaboration

Context Therapeutics — CT-95 — Pancreatic, Non-Small Cell Lung, Ovarian, Mesothelioma, Colorectal Cancer — Phase 1 Dose Escalation
Context Therapeutics — CT-202 — Bladder, Non-Small Cell Lung, Colorectal, Breast, Head and Neck Cancer — Preclinical Development
Celldex — Barzolvolimab — Chronic Spontaneous Urticaria — Commercial launch readiness funded by $300M offering.
Adagene — Muzastotug — Microsatellite Stable Colorectal Cancer — Clinical collaboration with Incyte for combination with INCA33890 bispecific.
Incyte — INCA33890 — Microsatellite Stable Colorectal Cancer — Phase 1 study with Muzastotug expected to begin in 2026.
Genmab — DuoBody/HexaBody — Oncology — Advancing late-stage clinical pipeline including bispecific antibody formats.
Zymeworks — Ziihera — HER2-positive cancers — Licensed asset under asset and royalty aggregation strategy.
Merck — Harpoon Therapeutics — Bispecific Antibodies — Acquisition
Merck — Infinimmune — Autoimmune Conditions — R&D Collaboration
Adagene — Muzastotug (ADG126) — Microsatellite Stable Colorectal Cancer — Clinical Collaboration with Incyte
Incyte — INCA33890 — Microsatellite Stable Colorectal Cancer — Combination Study with Muzastotug
Context Therapeutics — CT-95 — Solid Tumors — Phase 1 Dose Escalation
Context Therapeutics — CT-202 — Solid Tumors — Preclinical Development
Celldex — Bispecific Antibody Platform — Various — Platform growth funded by $300 million public offering.
Adagene — INCA33890 (TGFßR2xPD-1 Bispecific Antibody) — Microsatellite Stable Colorectal Cancer — Clinical Collaboration with Incyte initiated.
Zymeworks — Ziihera (zanidatamab-hrii) — HER2-positive cancers — Commercialization rights granted to BeOne Medicines and Jazz Pharmaceuticals.
Genmab — Bispecific Antibodies — Various — Ongoing advancement of late-stage clinical pipeline including bispecific formats.
Merck & Co.: Expanding biologics presence via acquisition and collaboration; associated with Harpoon Therapeutics and Infinimmune.
Infinimmune: Using AI to discover immune cell-directed antibodies; associated with IL-22 and IL-13 programs.
Harpoon Therapeutics: Developer of bispecific antibodies acquired by Merck.
Context Therapeutics — Advancing T cell engaging bispecific antibodies for solid tumors — CTIM-76, CT-95, CT-202
Adagene Inc. — Transforming discovery and development of novel antibody-based cancer immunotherapies — Muzastotug (ADG126)
Incyte — Developing TGFβR2xPD-1 Bispecific Antibody — INCA33890
Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc. — Producing optimized fully human antibodies and new classes of bispecific antibodies using VelociSuite — VelociSuite platform
Celldex Therapeutics: Fund commercial readiness for barzolvolimab and grow bispecific antibody platform via $300M capital raise.
Adagene Inc.: Transform discovery of novel antibody-based cancer immunotherapies via SAFEbody precision masking technology and Incyte collaboration.
Genmab A/S: Improve lives of people with cancer through innovative antibody medicines including bispecific antibodies and ADCs.
Zymeworks Inc.: Optimize cash flows from licensed products like Ziihera (bispecific) while developing novel multifunctional biotherapeutics.
Merck: Expanding biologics development presence to brace for Keytruda loss of exclusivity, associated with Harpoon Therapeutics and Infinimmune.
Infinimmune: Using AI to identify targets for future drugs, associated with IL-22 and IL-13 programs.
Context Therapeutics: Advancing T cell engaging bispecific antibodies for solid tumors including CTIM-76, CT-95, and CT-202.
Adagene: Transforming discovery and development of novel antibody-based cancer immunotherapies using SAFEbody precision masking technology.
Incyte: Developing TGFßR2xPD-1 bispecific antibody (INCA33890) for combination therapy in colorectal cancer.
Regeneron: Producing optimized fully human antibodies and new classes of bispecific antibodies using VelociSuite technology.
Celldex Therapeutics: Funding growth of bispecific antibody platform and clinical candidates via $300 million capital raise.
Adagene Inc.: Transforming discovery of novel antibody-based cancer immunotherapies including collaboration on INCA33890 bispecific antibody.
Genmab A/S: Improving lives of people with cancer through innovative antibody medicines including bispecific antibodies.
Zymeworks Inc.: Developing multifunctional biotherapeutics including HER2-targeted bispecific antibody Ziihera.
Infinimmune — AI-driven antibody discovery — Autoimmune conditions (atopic dermatitis, IgA nephropathy, ulcerative colitis) — Targets IL-22 and IL-13.

CTIM-76 — CLDN6 x CD3 bispecific TCE — Phase 1 — Ovarian, endometrial, or testicular cancer — Preliminary signs of anti-tumor activity including ongoing RECIST response observed; no CRS greater than Grade 1 observed
CT-95 — MSLN x CD3 bispecific TCE — Phase 1 — Pancreatic, non-small cell lung, ovarian, mesothelioma, colorectal cancer — Dose escalation ongoing toward target dose levels
INCA33890 — TGFβR2xPD-1 Bispecific Antibody — Phase 1 — Microsatellite Stable Colorectal Cancer — Study expected to begin in 2026 in 3L MSS CRC patients

CT-202 — Nectin-4 x CD3 bispecific TCE — Preclinical — Bladder, non-small cell lung, colorectal, breast, head and neck cancer — Regulatory filings completed to support initiation of first-in-human trial
Muzastotug: Phase 1b/2 study in late-line MSS CRC patients; combined with pembrolizumab showed 31% confirmed ORR in 20 mg/kg dose cohorts.
INCA33890: TGFßR2xPD-1 Bispecific Antibody; Phase 1 study expected in 2026 in 3L MSS CRC patients with and without liver metastases.
Barzolvolimab: Phase 2 studies in Chronic Spontaneous Urticaria and Cold Urticaria demonstrated rapid, profound, and durable improvements in patient quality of life (DLQI Domains).
ZW191: Antibody-drug conjugate targeting FRα; Phase 1 study (NCT06555744) assessing safety and preliminary anti-tumor activity in advanced solid tumors.
Ziihera: HER2-targeted bispecific antibody using Azymetric™ technology; licensed to BeOne Medicines and Jazz Pharmaceuticals.
Infinimmune targets IL-22 and IL-13 implicated in autoimmune conditions such as atopic dermatitis, IgA nephropathy and ulcerative colitis.
CTIM-76: CLDN6 x CD3 bispecific T cell engager; Phase 1 dose escalation; patients with ovarian, endometrial or testicular cancer; preliminary signs of anti-tumor activity observed, no CRS > Grade 1.
INCA33890 + Muzastotug: TGFßR2xPD-1 bispecific antibody + ADG126; Phase 1 study; patients with microsatellite stable colorectal cancer (MSS CRC); study expected to begin in 2026.
CT-95: MSLN x CD3 bispecific T cell engager; Phase 1 dose escalation; patients with pancreatic, non-small cell lung, ovarian, mesothelioma and colorectal cancer; interim data expected September 2026.

CT-202: Nectin-4 x CD3 bispecific T cell engager; Preclinical/Phase 1 initiation; patients with bladder, non-small cell lung, colorectal, breast, and head and neck cancer; first patient dosing expected Q3 2026.
INCA33890 — TGFßR2xPD-1 Bispecific Antibody — Phase 1 study expected to begin in 2026 — 3L MSS CRC patients — Combination with Muzastotug (ADG126).
Ziihera — HER2-targeted Bispecific Antibody — Commercialized/Partnered — HER2-expressing tumors — Utilizing Azymetric™ technology.
Muzastotug (ADG126) — SAFEbody precision masking technology — Phase 1b/2 — Late-line MSS CRC — 31% confirmed ORR in combined 20 mg/kg dose cohorts.
Barzolvolimab — Mast cell biology antibody — Phase 2/Commercial readiness — Chronic Spontaneous Urticaria (CSU) — Improvements seen across all six DLQI Domains.
Merck & Co. — Infinimmune — R&D collaboration worth up to $838 million in upfront and milestone payments.
Merck & Co. — Harpoon Therapeutics — Acquisition of bispecific antibody developer.
Adagene Inc. — Clinical Collaboration with Incyte — Evaluate Muzastotug with INCA33890 in MSS CRC
Context Therapeutics — Acquisition of CT-95 and in-licensing of CT-202 — Strengthened pipeline of TCE bispecific therapeutics
Adagene Inc. — Public Offering of US$70.0 Million ADSs — To fund operations into mid-2027
Celldex Therapeutics: $300 million public offering of common stock priced at $29.00 per share to fund operations and bispecific platform growth.
Adagene Inc.: $70 million public offering of ADSs priced at $3.75 per ADS to support clinical-stage biotechnology operations.
Adagene Inc. and Incyte: Clinical collaboration to evaluate Muzastotug with Incyte’s TGFßR2xPD-1 Bispecific Antibody INCA33890 in MSS CRC.
Zymeworks Inc.: Exclusive rights agreements with BeOne Medicines Ltd. and Jazz Pharmaceuticals Ireland Limited to develop and commercialize zanidatamab in different territories.
Genmab A/S: Orbis Investment Management Limited acquired 5.01% of share capital and voting rights as of March 31, 2026.
Merck and Infinimmune signed an agreement for an R&D collaboration worth as much as $838 million in upfront and milestone payments.
Merck purchased bispecific antibody developer Harpoon Therapeutics as part of efforts to expand biologics development.

Adagene and Incyte: Clinical collaboration to evaluate muzastotug (ADG126) in combination with Incyte’s TGFßR2xPD-1 bispecific antibody (INCA33890) in patients with microsatellite stable colorectal cancer.

Regeneron and TriNetX: Strategic collaboration to access de-identified electronic health records of 300 million patients to drive research and product development including bispecific antibody discovery.
Adagene: Pricing of US$70.0 million public offering of ADSs to support operations.
Context Therapeutics: Cash runway expected to fund operations into mid-2027 with $66.0 million cash and cash equivalents as of December 31, 2025.
Adagene — Incyte — Clinical Collaboration to evaluate Muzastotug with INCA33890 in MSS CRC.
Zymeworks — BeOne Medicines Ltd. — Exclusive rights to develop and commercialize zanidatamab in different territories.
Zymeworks — Jazz Pharmaceuticals Ireland Limited — Exclusive rights to develop and commercialize zanidatamab in different territories.
Celldex — Public Offering — $300 million common stock offering to fund bispecific platform growth.
Adagene — Public Offering — $70 million ADS offering to support pipeline.
Genmab — Orbis Investment Management — Acquired 5.01% stake in share capital and voting rights.
